parent_axis_record.name: theft_aggressiveness
parent_axis_record.def: 1: ignores property entirely · 2: notices objects in passing · 3: tracks valuables but doesn't act · 4: subtle attempts (sleight, lifting unattended things) · 5: actively pilfers everything not bolted down, brazen

hypothesis.def1: 1: ignores the environment · 5: meticulously probes every crevice for value
hypothesis.def2: 1: cautious or avoidant · 5: brazenly grabs items despite potential consequences
hypothesis.rationale: The variance appears to stem from 'romances-and-steals' measuring emotional desperation (high search intensity/frustration) while 'steals' measures stealthy professionalism (high audacity/low footprint).

hypothesis.def1: 1: overlooks hidden items · 5: actively probes for caches and loose scrap
hypothesis.def2: 1: purely reactive · 5: proactively snatches visible valuables without hesitation
hypothesis.rationale: This separates the methodical, searching behavior of a thief (scavenging) from the sudden, emotional outbursts of snatching items during social/emotional-heavy roleplay (impulse).

hypothesis.def1: 1: indifferent to objects · 5: obsessively focused on finding wealth
hypothesis.def2: 1: clumsy or uncoordinated · 5: efficient, practiced, and predatory
hypothesis.rationale: The 'steals' context measures successful or purposeful theft, while the 'romances-and-steals' context conflates high material intent (desperation/hunger) with erratic, low tactical success.
